title: Peace Talks on Hold: Trump Cancels Trip to Pakistan at the Last Minute
summary:
Have you ever planned something big and then changed your mind at the last second? That's what President Trump did on Saturday. He canceled a trip by his top negotiators to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran. The decision came just minutes after Iran's foreign minister had left Pakistan.

Trump said on his social media site, Truth Social, that he was canceling the trip. He told reporters that too much time is wasted on long airplane rides. "We have all the cards," he said. "We'll deal by telephone."

This all happened while there's a lot of fighting in the Middle East. Israel attacked targets in southern Lebanon, and the militant group Hezbollah fired rockets into Israel. That's despite a ceasefire (an agreement to stop fighting) that Trump said Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend by three weeks. But Hezbollah wasn't part of that deal and doesn't agree with it.

Iran says the ceasefire is "meaningless" because the U.S. still has a naval blockade (ships blocking Iranian ports). Iran says it won't return to talks until the blockade is lifted.

So what's next? Trump says Iran can call anytime. But with tensions so high, it's hard to know when peace will come. Stay tuned!
why_it_matters: When leaders cancel peace talks, it can mean more fighting and danger far away, which affects the safety and prices of things we buy even in our own country.

keywords.explanation: People chosen to discuss and try to reach an agreement with others.
An agreement to stop fighting for a period of time.
When ships or troops block a port to stop goods from entering or leaving.
Feelings of anger, fear, or stress between groups that could lead to conflict.
A person or group that uses force or violence to achieve political goals.
The government official in charge of a country's relationships with other countries.

options: Because he said too much time is wasted on long airplane rides

Where did President Trump announce the cancellation of the trip?
options: On his social media site Truth Social
On the phone with Pakistan's leader

background_read: Iran (a country in the Middle East) has been in conflict with the United States for many years over its nuclear program and support for militant groups.
Hezbollah (a powerful militant group based in Lebanon) often fights with Israel and is supported by Iran.
Pakistan (a country in South Asia) has sometimes helped the U.S. talk to other countries, but this time the talks were canceled at the last minute.
Article_Structure: WHO: President Donald Trump, Iran’s foreign minister, Israel, and the militant group Hezbollah.
WHAT: Trump canceled a trip by U.S. negotiators to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran, saying he prefers telephone calls.
WHERE: The canceled trip was to Pakistan; the fighting involves Israel, Lebanon, and Iran.
WHY: Trump said long airplane rides waste time, and he believes the U.S. has 'all the cards' in the negotiations.

perspectives.description: They think Trump is smart to avoid long trips and use phone calls instead, showing he is in control.
Iran feels the ceasefire is meaningless because of the U.S. naval blockade, and they refuse to talk until it is lifted.
They are caught in the middle of the fighting and hope for peace, but the canceled talks make peace less likely soon.
